macro_segments.title: Forced Get (Gett Me'useh): Cases Where a Get Is Valid or Invalid Based on Who Compels It and Whether Legally Mandated

Gemara Analysis: Rav Nachman's Rule—Get Valid If Legally Mandated, Invalid Otherwise (with Re'ach Haget Problem)
Rambam's Explanation: Beating Down Yetzer Hara to Free True Will to Do the Right Thing
Non-Jewish Court Forcing Get: Rav Marshisha's Position and Its Rejection
Chart of Four Cases: Legally Mandated/Non-Mandated × Jewish/Non-Jewish Court

macro_segments.title: Semicha and Authority: Who Is Empowered to Force a Get Without Ordination from Moshe Rabbeinu

micro_segments.title: Abaye Challenging Rav Yosef: Requirement for Semicha to Use Force and Impose Law
Tanna Requirement: Only Kosher Judges with Semicha Can Impose Law, Not Commoners (Hedyotot)
Agency of the Beit Din: Representing the Court with Semicha in Common Matters
Scope Debate: Common Matters (Hodaot, Halva'ot) vs. Theft, Injury, and Punishment (Knas)
Tosafot on Converting Gerim and Agency of Ancient Beit Din Without Living Semicha

macro_segments.title: Rumors About a Woman's Status: How Rumors of Marriage or Divorce Affect Her Halachic Status

micro_segments.title: Mishnah: If a Woman's Name Goes Out as Married or Divorced, She Is Treated Accordingly
Rav Ashi's Restriction: Rumors After Marriage Are Not Acted Upon; Only Before Marriage
Rumors of Promiscuity: Considered Mere Pritzuta (Loose Behavior), Not a Halachic Concern
Tannaitic Debate (Mechirat Socher): How Extreme Must Behavior Be to Suspect Adultery
Rabbi Yochanan ben Nuri Limits Scope: Two Witnesses Required, Not Rumors

macro_segments.title: Narrowing Rumor Concerns: Cases Where Rumors of Betrothal, Marriage, or Blemish Are Disregarded

micro_segments.title: Baraita: No Concern for Rumors of Sexual Relations, Marriage, Betrothal, Unnamed Person, or Other City
Baraita Continued: No Concern for Rumors of Mamzeret, Shifcha, Consecration, or Property Transfer
Ulla's Narrow Case: Rumor Valid Only with Circumstantial Evidence (Candles, Beds, People Entering/Exiting)
Gemara Clarification: Must Hear 'She Got Married Today,' Not 'She's Getting Married'
Gemara on Identifying Witnesses: Rumor Valid Only If Chain Leads Back to Named Witnesses

macro_segments.title: When to Nullify Rumors: Active Dispelling of Rumors and the Role of Explanations (Amtalot)

micro_segments.title: Abaye to Rav Yosef: Do We Actively Try to Dispel Rumors or Leave Them Alone?
Rav Chisda vs. Rav Sheshes: Disagreement About the Trustworthiness of Women as Sources
Regional Practice: Sura Nullifies Rumors; Nardah Does Not
Case of the Unripe Dates: Effective Nullification When Explanation Fits the Rumor's Own Logic
Case of Unknown Son: Nullification When Rumor Can Be Reinterpreted Without Contradicting Perception
Case of Minor Appearing Grown: No Nullification When Physical Reality Contradicts Explanation

macro_segments.title: Explanations (Amtalot) That Neutralize Rumors: When and How Explanations Can Invalidate a Rumor's Effect

micro_segments.title: Mishnah: Explanation Nullifies Rumor Only If No Amtala Present (e.g., Marriage on Condition)
Rabba bar Rav Huna vs. Rav Zeira: When Must Explanation Come—Same Day or Even Later?
Rav Zeira: Mere Possibility of Explanation (Bemakom Amtala) Neutralizes Rumor
Rav Kahana's Proof: Woman Married on One Witness to Dead Husband; Possibility of Tenai Permits Return
Gemara Limits Proof: Real-World Protest by Husband Is What Matters, Not Mere Explanation Possibility
Consequence Doctrine: Explanation Nullifies Only When No Issura (Prohibition) Yet Occurred
